title: Dragon Chicken

ingredients.name: boneless chicken breast or thigh, cut into thin strips

subs.name: 500 g paneer strips for a vegetarian Dragon Paneer
3 tbsp thick curd in the batter

ingredients.name: cornflour
visual_checks: 3 tbsp for batter, 1 tsp for sauce slurry
ingredients.notes: 3 tbsp for batter, 1 tsp for sauce slurry

subs.name: 30 g peanuts, fried until golden
subs.notes: mark peanut allergen instead of nuts

ingredients.name: spring onion, whites and greens separated

subs.name: 2 tbsp sriracha for a less complex heat

ingredients.name: oil
visual_checks: for deep frying plus 2 tbsp for stir-frying
ingredients.notes: for deep frying plus 2 tbsp for stir-frying

steps.text: In a bowl whisk together the egg, 3 tbsp cornflour, maida, ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chilli powder, black pepper and salt into a thick batter. Add the chicken strips and toss to coat thoroughly. Marinate for 15 minutes.

Heat oil in a kadai to 170 °C. Slide the chicken strips in batches of 6–8 and fry for 4 minutes until just cooked and pale gold. Drain. Raise the oil to 190 °C and fry the strips a second time for 2 minutes until very crispy. Drain on a rack.

In the same hot oil (reduce to 160 °C), fry the cashew halves for 90 seconds until golden. Remove immediately and drain — cashews continue cooking off the heat so pull them slightly before they look done.
Make a slurry with 1 tsp cornflour and 2 tbsp cold water. Combine soy sauce, schezwan sauce, ketchup, vinegar, remaining 1 tsp red chilli powder and sugar in a small bowl.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in the wok on the highest flame. Add spring onion whites and green chilli and toss for 20 seconds. Add onion and capsicum and stir-fry for 2 minutes until the edges char slightly.
Pour in the sauce blend and bring to a rapid boil. Add the cornflour slurry and stir for 30 seconds until the sauce turns thick and glossy.
Add the fried chicken strips and cashews and toss on high heat for 1 minute until everything is lacquered in the sauce. Finish with spring onion greens and serve immediately with fried rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → 500 g paneer strips for a vegetarian Dragon Paneer; cashew → 30 g peanuts, fried until golden (mark peanut allergen instead of nuts); egg → 3 tbsp thick curd in the batter; green-chilli-sauce → 2 tbsp sriracha for a less complex heat
